enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song 'Thaiya Thaiya'?

The song 'Thaiya Thaiya' expresses deep emotions of love and longing, encapsulating the beauty of romantic relationships.

What is the context of this song in the movie 'Uyire'?

In Uyire, 'Thaiya Thaiya' plays during a pivotal moment that highlights the romantic connection between the lead characters.

Who are the singers of 'Thaiya Thaiya'?

The song features the talented voices of Malgudi Subha, Palakkad Sriram, and Sukhwinder Singh.

Who composed the music for 'Thaiya Thaiya'?

The music for 'Thaiya Thaiya' was composed by the legendary AR Rahman, known for his innovative soundtracks.

When was 'Thaiya Thaiya' released?

'Thaiya Thaiya' was released on August 21, 1998, as part of the movie Uyire.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In 'Thaiya Thaiya', the characters express their profound love and connection through a beautifully crafted melody. The song captures the essence of romance, evoking feelings of nostalgia and joy. Set against the backdrop of Uyire, it enhances the emotional depth of the narrative. With its enchanting lyrics by Vairamuthu and the soulful composition by AR Rahman, this song remains a cherished classic in Tamil cinema.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Thaiya Thaiya from Uyire is a mesmerizing blend of soulful melodies and heartfelt lyrics that captures the essence of love. Featuring the enchanting voices of Malgudi Subha, Palakkad Sriram, and Sukhwinder Singh, this song is a testament to AR Rahman's genius in creating timeless music. Dive into the romantic vibes of this classic that continues to resonate with listeners today!
stanzaMeanings: The path of the wind... has the little bird chirping. Is the heart aware of the struggles? (2)

Her eyes are like two hundred moons, her beauty is like three hundred. To understand her beauty, one needs to live a hundred years, Thaiyyaa Thaiyyaa.
Just right, Thaiyyaa Thaiyyaa Thaiyyaa Thaiyyaa (4)
With one glance, you made me speak. With your small smile, you made me melt. (2)
Oh, you made my pride disappear. You made me work with your gaze.
You raised my hopes high, and with your feet, you made me yours. (2)
Before the rain comes to the earth, like that, the magical son has disappeared somewhere.
If I see, a meeting will happen; if you see, a connection will happen.
You are my beginning and my end. (3)
In a mountain, I saw a gem; my heart is your treasure. Will this last for a day or two, or will it touch the essence of life?

A rainbow does not come twice; it does not return once it has gone. A cuckoo that sings after crossing a dance does not lose its voice.
That singing voice does not lose its sound.
Because of you, my heart has settled halfway; because of you, my heart has wandered halfway. (2)
